# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

class LogsController < ApplicationController
    before_action :authenticate!, except: [:show] # Security requirement SECURE (DO THIS) - Authentication of admin users only for show action and all other actions are unauthorized. 
    
    def canonicalize(path)   #SECURITY REQUIREMENTS Canonicalizing file paths to prevent path traversal by removing any '..' segments from the filename or directory name, then normalising it (e.g., converting '/foo/../bar' into just 'bar'). 
        return nil if !path || path == "." || path == ".." # Reject absolute paths and directories starting with a dot-segment ('.').  
        `#{Rails.root}/logs/" + File.canonicalize(path) }' unless Rails.env.production?; end  //SECURE (DO THIS)" This line is used to canonicalise the file path and store it in a variable, then use that for reading log files
    def show   #INSECURE Do not rely on checking whether ".." appears directly inside filename or directory name unless explicitly allowed by user. 
        return head :not_found if !params[:log] || params[:log].include?("../") ||  File.directory?(filepath) == false;   #Reject traversal segments and directories outside the logs folder, rejecting any resolved path that is not within allowed directory (Rails root + log).
        send_file filepath  #Display content for debugging in a browser by sending it as an attachment. This action can be used to display large files or stream them directly from server memory into user's webbrowser, depending on the client requesting this resource and its capabilities (e.g., modern browsers).
    end  
    
private  #INSECURE Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in string unless explicitly allowed by users to display log files of other directories or filenames within logs directory, do no open file without validation if it is outside the defined base path. This part should be kept private and accessible for testing purposes but shouldn't expose publicly as they can lead into security issues
    def allowlist #SECURE (DO THIS) - A list of allowed log filenames or directories that are not traversal segments, must match exactly with the ones in logs directory. 
        @allowlisted_logs ||= Dir[Rails.root.join('log/*')].map { |p| File.basename(p) } #SECURE (DO THIS)" This line is used to get a list of allowed log filenames and store it in an instance variable, then use that for checking if the requested filename or directory name matches any on allowlist
    end 
end
